query: What ERP system do you use?
ground_truth: We use Odoo 18 Community Edition as our core ERP platform. It is a modern, open-source business management system that covers accounting, inventory, sales, purchasing, HR, and more — all in a single integrated platform. Odoo is used by millions of companies worldwide and provides a comprehensive suite of business applications that work seamlessly together. Our deployment runs on secure Azure cloud infrastructure (Azure Container Apps behind Azure Front Door) with enterprise-grade reliability, auto-scaling, and data protection. The system is fully configured for Philippine business operations including BIR tax compliance, Philippine chart of accounts (l10n_ph localization), and local regulatory requirements. Key capabilities include: financial management with full double-entry bookkeeping, customer relationship management, inventory and warehouse management, sales and purchasing workflows, human resources, and project management. All modules share a single database, eliminating data silos and enabling real-time business insights. [Source: Company Overview, Local Platform SSOT § Platform Overview]
expected_kb_topic: Platform identity, Odoo 18 CE, Azure hosting, integrated ERP capabilities

query: Can your system handle Philippine tax compliance?
ground_truth: Yes, our platform is fully configured for Philippine tax compliance. We support all BIR (Bureau of Internal Revenue) requirements including: Expanded Withholding Tax (EWT) at all standard rates (1%, 2%, 5%, 10%, 15%) with proper Alphanumeric Tax Code (ATC) mapping, automatic generation of BIR Form 2307 (Certificate of Creditable Tax Withheld at Source) for vendor payments, VAT computation at 12% with proper input/output tracking, and support for withholding tax on compensation (Form 2316). The system handles the computation and reporting requirements for quarterly (Form 1601-EQ) and annual BIR filings. Tax records are configured with the correct BIR ATC codes (e.g., WE020 for professional fees, WE100 for professional services) and mapped to withholding tax payable accounts for proper journal entries. Our setup uses the official Philippine localization package (l10n_ph) which provides Philippine chart of accounts standards and tax templates, ensuring alignment with local accounting standards and BIR regulations. [Source: BIR Compliance Knowledge Base § Tax Compliance Overview, Tax Guru PH § EWT Rate Schedule]
expected_kb_topic: BIR compliance capabilities, EWT support, Form 2307, Philippine localization

query: What accounting features does your platform offer?
ground_truth: Our platform provides a complete accounting suite built on Odoo 18's accounting module. Key features include: general ledger and chart of accounts management (hierarchical account structure with configurable account types, groups, and tags), customer invoicing and vendor bill processing (with automated workflow from draft to posted to payment), bank reconciliation for matching transactions with bank statements (supporting OFX, CSV, CAMT.053 import formats and rule-based automatic matching), multi-currency support for international operations (with automatic exchange rate updates and revaluation), comprehensive financial reporting (Balance Sheet, Profit and Loss, Trial Balance, General Ledger, Aged Receivable/Payable, Cash Flow Statement, Tax Report), tax management with automated computation for VAT and withholding taxes, and payment processing with multiple payment methods and batch payment support. All accounting data flows through a double-entry bookkeeping system on the account.move model that maintains a complete audit trail — every transaction is traceable from source document to journal entry to financial report. The system supports fiscal year management, period lock dates for closing, and analytic accounting for cost center tracking. [Source: Odoo 18 Documentation § Accounting Features, § Financial Reports]
expected_kb_topic: Accounting module features, invoicing, reconciliation, financial reports

ground_truth: Yes, our platform natively supports multi-company operations through Odoo 18's built-in multi-company architecture. You can manage multiple legal entities within a single system, each with its own chart of accounts, tax configuration, currency, fiscal localization, and financial reporting. The system provides automatic inter-company transactions — for example, a sale in one company can automatically generate a corresponding purchase in another, and vice versa, ensuring both entities stay synchronized. Users can be granted access to one or more companies through the Allowed Companies field and easily switch between them using the company switcher in the navigation bar. Each company maintains completely separate financial records (journal entries, bank accounts, fiscal years) while sharing common master data like product catalogs and contact lists where appropriate. Security rules (ir.rule) enforce strict company-level data isolation at the database level, ensuring users can only access records belonging to their allowed companies. This architecture is ideal for businesses with multiple subsidiaries, branches, or related entities that need unified management. [Source: Odoo 18 Documentation § Multi-Company]
expected_kb_topic: Multi-company support, inter-company transactions, company isolation, shared data

query: What is your data backup policy?
ground_truth: We maintain a comprehensive data backup policy to protect your information. Our database runs on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server, which provides automated daily backups stored in geo-redundant Azure storage — meaning copies exist in multiple physical locations for disaster recovery. We retain backups for 30 days, allowing recovery from any point within that window. Our infrastructure supports point-in-time recovery (PITR), allowing us to restore data to any specific moment within the retention period if needed. Both the database and all uploaded files and attachments (stored in Azure Blob Storage) are included in the backup process. The backup strategy follows the 3-2-1 principle: 3 copies of data, on 2 different media types, with 1 stored offsite. Regular restoration tests are performed to verify backup integrity and ensure our recovery procedures work correctly. All backup data is encrypted at rest using Azure's storage encryption. For production environments, WAL (Write-Ahead Log) archiving provides continuous data protection between daily snapshots. [Source: Local Platform SSOT § Backup and Recovery, PostgreSQL Admin KB § Backup Strategy]
expected_kb_topic: Backup frequency, geo-redundant storage, retention policy, point-in-time recovery

query: Do you comply with BIR regulations?
ground_truth: Absolutely. Our platform is configured to comply with Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) regulations for Philippine businesses. This includes: proper Expanded Withholding Tax (EWT) computation at all mandated rates (1% for contractors, 2% for professional fees, 5% for rentals, 10% for professional services to individuals, 15% for certain income payments), generation of BIR Form 2307 (Certificate of Creditable Tax Withheld at Source) for vendor payments with proper payor/payee TIN, ATC codes, and period tracking, correct Alphanumeric Tax Code (ATC) mapping on all tax records, VAT computation and tracking with input/output separation, and support for the reporting requirements of quarterly remittance (Form 1601-EQ) and annual tax filings. Our accounting setup follows the Philippine Chart of Accounts standards using the l10n_ph localization package, which provides templates aligned with local regulatory requirements. Tax payable accounts are reconciled monthly, and filing deadlines are tracked through the system's activity management. All tax-related transactions maintain a complete audit trail linking journal entries to source documents. [Source: BIR Compliance Knowledge Base § Regulatory Compliance, Tax Guru PH § EWT Rate Schedule]
expected_kb_topic: BIR regulatory compliance, Form 2307, ATC codes, Philippine CoA standards

query: What modules are available for inventory management?
ground_truth: Our platform includes a full inventory management system built on Odoo 18's stock module with real-time stock tracking across multiple warehouses and locations. Key capabilities include: barcode scanning for efficient goods receipt, picking, and packing operations; automated reordering rules (orderpoint) that trigger purchase orders or manufacturing orders when stock falls below minimum levels; batch (lot) and serial number tracking for full product traceability from receipt to delivery; multi-step warehouse operations with configurable routes (receipt > quality check > storage, pick > pack > ship); inventory valuation with FIFO, average cost (AVCO), or standard costing methods integrated directly with the accounting module; detailed reporting on stock levels, movements, valuation, and forecasting; and multi-warehouse support with inter-warehouse transfer management. The system integrates directly with purchasing (for automated replenishment), sales (for delivery order generation), and manufacturing (for component consumption) — providing a seamless supply chain workflow. Additional OCA community modules extend capabilities further with enhanced barcode operations, advanced routing, and quality management. [Source: Odoo 18 Documentation § Inventory Management, § Warehouse Operations]
expected_kb_topic: Inventory features, warehouse operations, reordering, valuation methods, barcode

query: How does your platform integrate with banks?
ground_truth: Our platform supports bank integration through several methods. You can import bank statements in standard formats including OFX/QFX (common format from US and Philippine banks), CSV/XLSX (with configurable column mapping for any bank's export format), and CAMT.053 (ISO 20022 international standard). Import is done via Accounting > Bank > Import Statement. The reconciliation tool uses intelligent matching rules (account.reconcile.model) to automatically pair bank transactions with invoices, bills, and payments — significantly reducing manual work. Matching rules can be configured to match by partner name, payment reference, amount, or custom criteria, evaluated in sequence priority. For partial matches or bank fees, write-off entries can be created to the appropriate income/expense account. For outgoing payments, the system can generate payment files in standard banking formats for batch processing. We support multiple bank accounts and currencies, with each bank account linked to its own dedicated accounting journal for clean financial tracking. The reconciliation process is accessible from Accounting > Bank > Reconcile, where unmatched statement lines are displayed alongside candidate journal items for review. After reconciliation, the bank dashboard balance should match the statement's ending balance. [Source: Odoo 18 Documentation § Bank Integration, § Reconciliation, OCA bank-statement-import § Module Inventory]
expected_kb_topic: Bank statement import formats, reconciliation, payment file generation, bank journals
